2605.29560.title: Battery-Sim-Agent: Leveraging LLM-Agent for Inverse Battery Parameter Estimation
2605.29560.paper_summary: Parameterizing high-fidelity "digital twins" of batteries is a critical yet challenging inverse problem that hinders the pace of battery innovation. Prevailing methods formulate this as a black-box optimization (BBO) task, employing algorithms that are sample-inefficient and blind to the underlying physics. In this work, we introduce a new paradigm that reframes the inverse problem as a reasoning task, and present Battery-Sim-Agent, the first framework to deploy a Large Language Model (LLM) agent in a closed loop with a high-fidelity battery simulator. The agent mimics a human scientist's workflow: it interprets rich, multi-modal feedback from the simulator, forms physically-grounded hypotheses to explain discrepancies, and proposes structured parameter updates. On a systematically constructed benchmark suite spanning diverse battery chemistries, operating conditions, and difficulty levels, our agent significantly outperforms strong BBO baselines like Bayesian optimization in identifying accurate parameters. We further demonstrate the framework's capability in complex long-horizon degradation fitting tasks and validate its practical applicability on real-world battery datasets. Our results highlight the promise of LLM-agents as reasoning-based optimizers for scientific discovery and battery parameter estimation.
2605.29560.paper_summary_zh: 參數化高保真度的「數位雙胞胎」電池是一個關鍵但具挑戰性的反向問題，這妨礙了電池創新的進程。現行方法將其表述為黑箱優化（BBO）任務，採用的算法在樣本效率上表現不佳，且對潛在物理原理視而不見。在本研究中，我們引入了一種新的範式，將反向問題重新框架為推理任務，並提出了Battery-Sim-Agent，這是第一個在高保真電池模擬器中以閉環方式部署大型語言模型（LLM）代理的框架。該代理模擬了人類科學家的工作流程：它解釋來自模擬器的豐富多模態反饋，形成基於物理的假設來解釋差異，並提出結構化的參數更新。在一個系統性構建的基準套件中，涵蓋了多樣的電池化學、操作條件和難度級別，我們的代理在識別準確參數方面顯著超越了像貝葉斯優化這樣的強BBO基準。我們進一步展示了該框架在複雜長期退化擬合任務中的能力，並驗證了其在現實世界電池數據集上的實用性。我們的結果突顯了LLM代理作為基於推理的優化器在科學發現和電池參數估計中的潛力。
2605.29560.author: Jiawei Chen et.al.
2605.29560.authors: Jiawei Chen, Xiaofan Gui, Shikai Fang, Shengyu Tao, Shun Zheng, Weiqing Liu, Jiang Bian
